theme: Emotional Intelligence — Make the Office Feel Your Pain and Celebrate Your Wins

ideas.title: Favicon Status Badge — The Tab Speaks
ideas.description: The browser tab favicon never changes. When workers are active, swap to a green-dot favicon. When errors exist, swap to a red-dot. When idle/after-hours, use a dim version. Implementation: generate 3 canvas-drawn favicons on mount (green/red/dim circles overlaid on the blob emoji), swap via document.querySelector('link[rel=icon]').href based on worker state. WHY: Power users have 30+ tabs open. A dynamic favicon is the ONLY way to communicate status without the tab being visible. Discord, Slack, Gmail all do this. It's table stakes for any real-time dashboard.

ideas.title: Tab Title Pulse — Show Life in the Browser Tab
ideas.description: The page title is static 'Bloberto Office'. When workers are active, cycle the title every 8s: '🟢 2 working · Bloberto Office' → '⚙️ Carlos on Backend · Bloberto Office'. When errors: '🔴 Error! · Bloberto Office'. When idle: '😴 All quiet · Bloberto Office'. Implementation: useEffect with setInterval that updates document.title. WHY: Combined with favicon, this makes the tab a mini-dashboard. Users who background the tab still get ambient awareness. Zero visual cost — it's metadata.

ideas.title: Worker Completion Celebration — Confetti Per Character
ideas.description: Confetti currently fires generically. When a specific worker completes, their confetti should use their role color. Maya gets purple/pink, Carlos gets blue/steel, Dave gets orange/amber. Implementation: pass the completing worker's role-color to the confetti spawner, use it for 60% of pieces (rest random). Also show a brief toast: '🎉 Maya shipped it!' that fades after 3s. WHY: Generic celebrations feel hollow. Personalized ones feel like the app actually knows who did the work. It's the difference between a form letter and a handwritten note.

ideas.title: Coffee Break Animation — Workers Visit the Coffee Corner
ideas.description: The coffee corner exists as a static decoration. When a worker transitions from 'working' to 'idle', briefly animate their character walking to the coffee corner (2s path animation) before settling into their wander path. Show a tiny steam emoji above the corner when someone visits. Implementation: add a 'coffee-break' transition state that plays a CSS path animation from desk position to coffee corner, then switches to idle wander. WHY: Real offices have coffee runs between tasks. This 2-second micro-story makes status transitions feel like life events instead of state machine flips.

ideas.title: Keyboard Shortcuts — Power User Express Lane
ideas.description: No keyboard shortcuts exist. Add: 'D' for dashboard tab, 'O' for office tab, 'R' for manual refresh/sync, '?' to show shortcut overlay. Implementation: single useEffect keydown listener on document, switch on e.key, call existing tab/sync functions. Shortcut overlay is a fixed div with the key mappings. WHY: Anyone who leaves this tab open all day is a power user. Power users expect keyboard shortcuts. It costs 30 lines of JS and makes the app feel like a real tool, not a toy.

ideas.title: Activity Log Grouping by Worker — See Each Person's Day
ideas.description: Activity log shows events chronologically which is fine for real-time, but makes it hard to answer 'what did Maya do today?' Add a toggle/filter to group by worker: click a worker name in the filter bar to see only their events. Implementation: extend the existing activity-filter-bar with worker-name buttons derived from unique workers in the log. Filter logic: activityLog.filter(e => e.worker === selected). WHY: The log is getting long enough that scrolling for one person's history is tedious. This is the natural next evolution of the filter system that already exists for event types.

ideas.title: Office Ambient Sound Toggle — Hear the Vibe
ideas.description: Add a tiny speaker icon in the header. Click to toggle ambient sound: keyboard clicks when workers are active, soft rain for after-hours, upbeat lo-fi for crushing-it vibe. Implementation: Web Audio API with 3-4 short looping samples (~50KB each), volume tied to worker count. Muted by default, opt-in only. WHY: This is the nuclear option for immersion. Nobody expects a dashboard to have sound. The surprise factor alone would make people share it. But it MUST be opt-in and muted by default or it's a war crime.
